message:
chore: update VEF adversarial results, proof scheduler conformance, and beads metadata

- Refresh VEF adversarial test results with latest evaluation data
- Update bd-3ptu verification evidence and summary for Section 10.18
- Enhance VEF adversarial suite check script and security test scenarios
- Add VEF proof scheduler support conformance test
- Update beads issue tracker with latest task state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2zz","title":"[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.1 — Charter + Split Governance

Why This Exists:
Program charter and governance baseline: split contracts, reproducibility, and anti-regression rules for strategic integrity.

Task Objective:
Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement with explicit success/failure invariants and deterministic behavior under normal, edge, and fault scenarios.
- Ensure outcomes are verifiable via automated tests and reproducible artifact outputs.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-owned design/spec artifact at `docs/specs/section_10_1/bd-2zz_contract.md`, capturing decision rationale, invariants, and interface boundaries.
- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_1/bd-2zz/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_1/bd-2zz/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.1] Add dependency-direction guard preventing local engine crate reintroduction." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:43.448201283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T09:08:43.244168356Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T09:08:43.244143199Z","close_reason":"Dependency-direction guard implemented. 4 checks (workspace members, package names, dependency direction, crates dir) all PASS. 9 unit tests all pass. Spec, guard script, and verification artifacts created.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-1","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zz","depends_on_id":"bd-1j2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:10.617269548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3014","title":"[10.15] Integrate canonical remote named-computation registry (from `10.14`) for control-plane distributed actions.","description":"## Why This Exists
Hard Runtime Invariant #6 from Section 8.5 requires a remote effects contract: all distributed actions must go through a named-computation registry that rejects unknown computation identifiers fail-closed. Section 10.14 (bd-ac83) built the canonical remote named-computation registry as a core asupersync primitive. This bead integrates that canonical registry into franken_node's control-plane layer so that distributed actions (remote health probes, cross-node rollout coordination, distributed fencing token operations, remote migration steps) use the same registry semantics. The key constraint is "no divergent registry behavior" — the product layer must not create a parallel registry or bypass the canonical one. Unknown computation names must fail closed with a stable error class, not silently fall through to a default handler.

## What This Must Do
1. Author `docs/integration/control_remote_registry_adoption.md` defining:
   - Which control-plane distributed actions exist (from bd-2177 workflow inventory).
   - How each maps to a named computation in the canonical registry (computation name, input schema, output schema, timeout, retry policy).
   - The fail-closed contract: any computation name not registered returns `RemoteComputationUnknown` error (stable error class, not a generic error).
   - The prohibition on divergent registries: no module may maintain a parallel name->handler map.
2. Integrate the canonical registry into control-plane modules:
   - `crates/franken-node/src/connector/` modules that perform remote operations must look up computation handlers through the canonical registry.
   - Add registration of control-plane computations at startup (lifecycle.rs init phase).
   - Wire the registry's fail-closed rejection into error handling paths.
3. Implement `tests/conformance/named_remote_computations.rs` that:
   - Asserts all control-plane remote operations use the canonical registry.
   - Attempts to invoke an unregistered computation name and asserts `RemoteComputationUnknown` error.
   - Asserts no divergent registry exists in the crate (module scan).
4. Generate `artifacts/10.15/remote_registry_adoption_report.json` with: registered computation names, usage sites, unregistered-name rejection test results.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Control-plane paths use the same canonical registry semantics as 10.14; unknown names fail closed with stable error class; no divergent registry behavior is introduced.
- Every remote operation in the control-plane is registered by name in the canonical registry.
- The `RemoteComputationUnknown` error class has a stable error code that does not change across versions.
- No module under `crates/franken-node/src/connector/` maintains its own computation name->handler mapping.
- The adoption report is consumed by the section gate (bd-20eg).

## Testing & Logging Requirements
- **Unit tests**: Validate computation registration, lookup, and fail-closed rejection with mock registry.
- **Integration tests**: Start the connector, register computations, invoke each registered computation, and assert success. Invoke an unregistered name and assert fail-closed.
- **Conformance tests**: Scan all modules for any `HashMap<String, Handler>` or similar patterns that could be a parallel registry.
- **Adversarial tests**: Attempt to register a computation with a name collision; assert rejection. Attempt to bypass the registry by directly constructing a remote call; assert compile-time or runtime rejection.
- **Structured logs**: Event codes `RMT-001` (computation registered), `RMT-002` (computation invoked), `RMT-003` (unknown computation rejected), `RMT-004` (divergent registry detected). Include computation_name, caller_module, and trace correlation ID.

## Expected Artifacts
- `docs/integration/control_remote_registry_adoption.md`
- `tests/conformance/named_remote_computations.rs`
- `artifacts/10.15/remote_registry_adoption_report.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-3014/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-3014/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies
- **Upstream**: bd-ac83 (10.14 — canonical remote named-computation registry implementation)
- **Downstream**: bd-20eg (section gate)","acceptance_criteria":"- Control-plane paths use the same canonical registry semantics as `10.14`; unknown names fail closed with stable error class; no divergent registry behavior is introduced.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:00.138232005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:01:43.741885689Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:01:43.741856054Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3014","depends_on_id":"bd-ac83","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T14:59:47.584707824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-31tg","title":"[15] Pillar: partner and lighthouse programs","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 15

Task Objective:
Implement partner/lighthouse adoption programs proving category-shift outcomes.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [15] Pillar: partner and lighthouse programs are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [15] Pillar: partner and lighthouse programs are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_15/bd-31tg/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_15/bd-31tg/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[15] Pillar: partner and lighthouse programs" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[15] Pillar: partner and lighthouse programs" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Partner program defined with tiers: (a) Lighthouse (early adopters, deep integration, direct support channel), (b) Partner (validated migration, co-marketing), (c) Community (self-service, public resources).
2. Lighthouse program has >= 3 active participants running franken_node in production or staging.
3. Each lighthouse partner has: (a) documented use case, (b) migration case study (published or in-progress), (c) feedback channel with response SLA <= 48 hours, (d) quarterly review cadence.
4. Partner program has clear entry criteria: minimum project size, commitment to provide feedback, willingness to publish anonymized results.
5. Benefits documented: priority bug fixes, early access to features, co-marketing opportunities, benchmark co-design input.
6. Program produces >= 2 published case studies within first 6 months.
7. Partner satisfaction tracked via quarterly survey; target NPS >= 30.
8. Evidence: partner_program_status.json with partner count by tier, case study list, and satisfaction scores.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:36.527116261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T06:41:40.978282557Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T06:41:40.978258742Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31tg","depends_on_id":"bd-1961","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:26.385358774Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-32e","title":"Implement init command with profile bootstrapping","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md (Bootstrap bridge for Sections 10.0 and 10.1 operator onboarding)
Section: BOOTSTRAP (CLI onboarding bridge)

Task Objective:
Implement `franken-node init` with profile bootstrapping so first-time operators can create deterministic, policy-aligned project configuration safely.

In Scope:
- `init` command flow to generate baseline config/profile files.
- Deterministic profile template selection and safe overwrite semantics.
- Clear onboarding diagnostics for missing prerequisites and invalid target states.

Acceptance Criteria:
- `init` produces deterministic output files for equivalent inputs/options.
- Existing configuration handling is explicit (confirm/abort/backup semantics) and non-destructive by default.
- Generated profile artifacts are immediately consumable by downstream run/doctor flows.

Expected Artifacts:
- Init flow contract note with option matrix and file-output expectations.
- Generated fixture snapshots for representative init scenarios.
- Machine-readable init summary artifact for CI assertions.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_bootstrap/bd-32e/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_bootstrap/bd-32e/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for template rendering, path handling, and overwrite-policy logic.
- Integration tests validating end-to-end init behavior in clean and pre-existing directories.
- E2E tests simulating first-run operator onboarding workflows.
- Structured logs containing init phase events, file actions, and policy decisions with trace IDs.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "Implement init command with profile bootstrapping", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "Implement init command with profile bootstrapping" must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "Implement init command with profile bootstrapping" must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "Implement init command with profile bootstrapping" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "Implement init command with profile bootstrapping" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","notes":"Taking over bootstrap init implementation after bd-1pk closure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:29:25.296726699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:47:21.555255072Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:47:21.555227570Z","close_reason":"Completed: init bootstrap overwrite/backup policy + contract gate + evidence","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","config"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32e","depends_on_id":"bd-3rp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:29:39.546835453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32e","depends_on_id":"bd-3vk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:04:16.558688526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32e","depends_on_id":"bd-n9r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:29:39.613041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-32p","title":"[PLAN 10.18] Verifiable Execution Fabric Execution Track (9L)","description":"Section: 10.18 — Verifiable Execution Fabric Execution Track (9L)

Strategic Context:
VEF track for proof-carrying runtime compliance: receipt chains, commitment checkpoints, proof generation/verification, and claim gating.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.18] Verifiable Execution Fabric Execution Track (9L)" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff documentation.","notes":"Acceptance Criteria alias (plan-space normalization, 2026-02-20):
- The `Success Criteria` section in this bead is the canonical acceptance contract for closure.
- Closure additionally requires linked unit/integration/E2E verification evidence and detailed structured logging artifacts from all child implementation
